principle: Text has 4 quality levels: action, words, sentences, structure
explanation:
text.quality == 4 levels >> all must work together
lvl1: useful_action == reader gets what they need
lvl2: words == NO language_garbage, NO stumble_words
lvl3: sentences+paragraphs == easy scan, visual anchor, no fog
lvl4: structure == supports text_goal, not contradicts it
NO: fix only words >> text still broken at action level
eg: SEO article -> lvl1=answers_query, lvl2=no_fluff, lvl3=scannable, lvl4=intro+body+CTA
WHEN: any text eval -> check all 4 levels top-down

principle: Start with reader needs, not your message
explanation:
text.goal == what reader needs >> NOT what you want to say
ask: why will reader read this? >> answer shapes structure
NO: write what we want to tell >> reader gets nothing useful
eg(BAD): company_site sections=[history, values, suppliers, team]
eg(GOOD): company_site sections=[materials, ergonomics, waterproofing, hardware]
rule: reader_query -> content_answer >> NOT company_pride -> content
WHEN: planning any page/section -> ask 'what does reader need here?' FIRST
SEO_link: search_intent == reader_need >> match both or rank drops

principle: Different readers = different content on separate pages
explanation:
audience.segment -> dedicated_page >> NOT one page tries all
eg: backpack_site -> buyers=features, employees=culture, investors=financials, suppliers=terms
NO: mix all audiences on homepage >> everyone gets nothing
rule: identify_audience -> map_their_needs -> separate_content
SEO_link: one_page_one_audience == one_search_intent >> better ranking + relevance
WHEN: designing site structure -> segment audiences FIRST, then create pages

principle: Useful action: text must fulfill its promise to reader
explanation:
useful_action == text_promises_X >> text_delivers_X
NO: text_promises_help >> delivers company_history
NO: complexity_mismatch -> terms reader doesn't know + no explanation
rule: set_useful_action FIRST >> all structure/words serve it
eg: landing_page -> useful_action=convince_buy -> structure=benefits+proof+CTA
eg: how-to article -> useful_action=reader_can_do_it -> structure=steps+examples
WHEN: any text fails -> check useful_action defined? fulfilled?
SEO: page_intent == useful_action >> Google ranks intent-match pages higher

principle: Stop-words: language garbage that carries no meaning
explanation:
stop_words == words with NO semantic value >> remove always
NO: 'безусловно, нанообработка поверхностей является на сегодняшний день наилучшим способом'
eg(GOOD): 'кремниевая пропитка — лучший способ защиты от влаги'
pattern: remove -> re-read -> meaning unchanged? word was garbage
stop_word_types: [filler_adverbs, bureaucratic_nouns, vague_adjectives, empty_intros]
WHEN: editing any text -> pass 1: kill stop-words only
SEO_link: stop_words dilute keyword_density >> remove to improve signal

principle: Edit direction: top-down for new text, bottom-up for existing
explanation:
new_text: start=structure -> fill=paragraphs -> refine=sentences -> clean=words
existing_text: start=words(stop_words) -> up=sentences -> up=paragraphs -> up=structure
NO: start editing words on broken structure >> wasted effort
rule: diagnose_level_of_problem -> start editing at THAT level
eg: text_unclear -> check structure FIRST before fixing words
WHEN: own writing -> top-down; editing others -> bottom-up

principle: Good text = clean room: remove trash, arrange what remains
explanation:
text_quality == 2 steps: remove_garbage + arrange_what_stays
NO: only remove words >> still messy structure
NO: only rearrange >> garbage stays
metaphor: room=text, furniture=content, trash=stop_words, layout=structure
result: reader enters -> knows where everything is -> no stumbling
WHEN: reviewing text quality -> ask 'is the garbage gone?' AND 'is what remains well-arranged?'

principle: Sentences and paragraphs: visual scan must work without reading
explanation:
para.quality == reader_scans_page -> gets structure WITHOUT full read
require: visual_anchors=headings/bold, short_paragraphs, logical_breaks
NO: wall_of_text -> reader skips entire block
NO: fog == reader cannot picture what is described
rule: test=close_eyes -> describe what you read -> if fuzzy -> rewrite
eg: product_description -> concrete_specs beat vague_adjectives always
WHEN: web/landing content -> optimize for scan FIRST, then read
SEO: scannable_content == lower_bounce_rate -> positive ranking signal

principle: Structure serves the text goal, never contradicts it
explanation:
structure.rule: text_goal -> structure_supports_it >> NOT structure_from_template
NO: ad_goal=convince_buy >> structure=company_history+values
eg(GOOD): ad_goal=convince_buy -> structure=benefits+social_proof+CTA
eg(GOOD): article_goal=teach -> structure=problem+solution+practice
test: for each section ask 'does this section move reader toward text_goal?'
NO section -> cut it
WHEN: planning any content -> write goal FIRST, then build structure around it
